# Card Combat Engine

A headless, domain-agnostic turn-based card combat engine for **Godot 4.6 /
GDScript**. Handles the *logic* of a card battle — turn FSM, mana, draw,
attack/defense/block, damage resolution, and pluggable AI — and exposes every
game-specific rule as an injectable `Callable`.

> **Core idea:** the engine is a pure logic layer. It knows nothing about your
> GDD, rarities, or abilities. You inject the specifics; it runs the match.

## Overview

Card Combat Engine fills the gap between Godot's presentation-layer card addons
(drag, animate, layout) and a working rule set. It provides the *brain*, not the
visuals.

**What it does:** turn FSM (`BEGIN → PREPARATION → MAIN → ATTACK → DEFENSE →
RESOLVE → END`), mana ramp, hand/board/graveyard zones, creature combat,
spell resolution, a structured event log, full serialization for save/resume and
server-authoritative netcode.

**What it doesn't do:** rendering, UI, card loaders, rarities, named abilities,
or game-specific balance. All of that is injected from your game layer.

**Determinism:** `CombatSession.setup(..., ai_seed)` seeds shuffles and the AI.
Same seed + same starting decks = bit-for-bit identical combat — ideal for
replays and authoritative networking.

**Topology:** supports 1v1, 2v2, and N-sided FFA via `setup_sides(sides, teams)`.

## Documentation

- [Addon README](addons/card_combat/README.md) — full class reference, all
  injection points, signal catalog, serialization protocol, AI contract, replay
  pattern, and observability guide.
- [Integration Guide](addons/card_combat/docs/integration_guide.md) — 12-step
  walkthrough: minimum setup → AbilityLibrary → custom abilities → human input →
  custom AI → save/resume → multi-side → networking.
- [Tutorial](addons/card_combat/docs/tutorial.md) — build *Tiny Clash* from zero:
  headless first, then keyword abilities, custom abilities, live signals, and a
  human-driven turn.
- [Examples](addons/card_combat/examples/) — `demo.tscn` (DummyAI vs DummyAI,
  full log), `custom_ai.gd` (AI contract skeleton), `ability_demo.gd`
  (AbilityLibrary wiring).

## Architecture

Three ideas carry the whole engine:

1. **The engine is agnostic.** It understands decks, mana, a turn FSM,
   attacking, blocking and damage — nothing about your game's rarities,
   elements, or named abilities.
2. **You inject the specifics.** Game rules enter through `Callable` hooks set
   on `CombatSession` before `setup()`, and through the opaque
   `CardData.metadata: Dictionary`. The engine never reads `metadata`; your code
   does.
3. **You drive the FSM.** For AI-vs-AI call `auto_resolve()`. For a human player
   react to the `phase_changed` signal and call the action methods
   (`play_card`, `declare_attacker`, `declare_blocker`, …).

**`CombatSession`** is the single coordinator: FSM, mana, draw, damage
resolution, event log, command log, and serialization all live there by design
(extracted only when the extraction adds value — see the addon README for the
reasoning).

**`CardData.play_kind`** controls engine behavior: `UNIT` (persists on board,
combats), `EFFECT` (resolves spell effects, goes to graveyard), `PERSISTENT`
(persists on board, triggers auras, does not combat). It is a dispatch enum,
not a game taxonomy — game taxonomies (Weapon, Terrain, Rarity) belong in
`metadata`.

## Key files

| File | Role |
|------|------|
| `addons/card_combat/combat_session.gd` | Main coordinator — FSM, actions, serialization |
| `addons/card_combat/combat_deck.gd` | Card zones (hand / draw pile / board / graveyard / mana / extra) |
| `addons/card_combat/card_data.gd` | Card core (id, cost, stats, `play_kind`, `metadata`) |
| `addons/card_combat/card_instance.gd` | In-play card (health, modifiers, triggers) |
| `addons/card_combat/combatant.gd` | Hero / participant (health, damage, heal, signals) |
| `addons/card_combat/combat_ai.gd` | AI contract (5 methods every AI must implement) |
| `addons/card_combat/dummy_ai.gd` | Reference AI (seeded random) |
| `addons/card_combat/heuristic_ai.gd` | Stronger AI (greedy curve-filling, value trades) |
| `addons/card_combat/spell_effect.gd` | Spell effect descriptor (DAMAGE/HEAL/BUFF/AOE/SUMMON/custom) |
| `addons/card_combat/combat_config.gd` | Balance parameters (mana caps, hand/board limits) |
| `addons/card_combat/combat_trigger_queue.gd` | Deferred trigger FIFO (QUEUED trigger mode) |
| `addons/card_combat/hidden_card_stats.gd` | Declared vs hidden stats for bluffing |
| `addons/card_combat/abilities/ability_library.gd` | Opt-in keyword ability handler |
| `test/` | GUT 9.6 test suite — 418 cases across 20 files |
| `addons/card_combat/examples/demo.tscn` | Runnable smoke-check demo |
| `addons/card_combat/benchmark/combat_benchmark.gd` | Performance / leak measurement |

## Injection points

All hooks are `Callable` properties set on `CombatSession` **before** `setup()`.
Every hook is optional — omit any you don't need.

| Property | Signature | Purpose |
|----------|-----------|---------|
| `ability_fn` | `(inst: CardInstance, trigger: int, ctx: Dictionary)` | Ability trigger dispatch (called by the engine for every card event; `trigger` is a `CardInstance.Trigger` enum value, `inst` is `null` for the side-level `ON_DRAW`/`ON_CAST`) |
| `damage_fn` | `(attacker: CardInstance, defender: CardInstance) -> int` | Combat damage formula override |
| `exhaust_fn` | `(owner_id: int)` | Called when a side's draw pile empties (fatigue) |
| `discard_fn` | `(card: CardData, owner_id: int)` | Called when a card is discarded |
| `attack_restriction_fn` | `(attacker: CardInstance, enemies: Array) -> Array` | Filter valid attack targets (TAUNT redirect) |
| `incoming_damage_fn` | `(inst: CardInstance, amount: int, source) -> int` | Pre-damage interception (armor, prevention) |
| `cost_fn` | `(card: CardData, owner_id: int) -> int` | Dynamic card cost override |
| `spell_power_fn` | `(owner_id: int) -> int` | Spell damage bonus |
| `aura_fn` | `(session: CombatSession)` | Called to recompute continuous modifiers (auras) |

Two `CombatConfig` flags complete the observability surface: `record_events`
(default `true`; set `false` in mass balancing runs to skip the `event_log`
appends — live signals still fire) and `emit_action_rejections` (default `true`;
gates the `action_rejected(action, reason)` signal a UI can listen to for
machine-readable feedback on why a driver action was refused).

## AbilityLibrary keywords

`AbilityLibrary` is an **opt-in** module that provides 14 ready-made ability
keywords. Wire it once and declare abilities in `CardData.metadata["keywords"]`:

```gdscript
var session := CombatSession.new()
var lib := AbilityLibrary.new(session)
lib.wire_all()   # sets ability_fn, attack_restriction_fn, incoming_damage_fn,
                 # spell_power_fn, and aura_fn on the session
```

| Keyword | Behavior | Metadata key (if tunable) |
|---------|----------|---------------------------|
| `CHARGE` | Can attack the turn it enters play | — |
| `IMMUNITY` | Absorbs N hits completely | `immunity_hits` (default 1, -1 = infinite) |
| `LIFESTEAL` | Combat damage heals the owner's hero | — |
| `TAUNT` | Enemies must attack this creature while alive | — |
| `THORNS` | Returns N damage to the attacker on hit | `thorns` (default 1) |
| `STEALTH` | Cannot be attacked until it attacks | — |
| `WINDFURY` | Can attack N times per turn | `windfury_attacks` (default 2) |
| `FREEZE` | Freezes damaged creatures for N turns | `freeze_turns` (default 1) |
| `ARMOR` | Reduces each incoming hit by N | `armor` (needs `incoming_damage_fn`) |
| `BATTLECRY` | On play: deals N damage to a chosen target | `battlecry_damage` (default 1) |
| `SPELLPOWER` | Adds N to the owner's spell damage | `spell_power` (needs `spell_power_fn`) |
| `LORD` | Aura: boosts other friendly creatures +N/+M | `aura_attack`, `aura_health` (default 1) |
| `OVERKILL` | Excess lethal damage transfers to the enemy hero | `overkill_factor` (default 1) |
| `SPELLBURST` | Gains +N/+M permanent when owner casts a spell | `spellburst_attack`, `spellburst_health` |

## Quick start

```gdscript
# Minimum 1v1 (AI vs AI, headless)
var session := CombatSession.new()
session.setup(hero0, deck0, hero1, deck1)
session.auto_resolve()   # drives the whole combat (calls start() itself)
var result := session.get_result()   # {winner_side, winner_team, turn_number, hp}

# With AbilityLibrary (wire BEFORE setup so initial draws see the handler)
var lib := AbilityLibrary.new(session)
lib.wire_all()   # sets session.ability_fn = lib.ability_handler, plus the other 4 hooks

# Human-driven turn (react to signals)
session.phase_changed.connect(func(old, new): _on_phase(new))
session.start()

func _on_phase(phase: int) -> void:
    if phase == CombatState.Phase.MAIN and session.active_side == PLAYER_SIDE:
        session.play_card(hand_card)
        session.end_main_phase()
```

Full working example: `addons/card_combat/examples/demo.tscn`.

## Testing

```bash
godot --headless -s addons/gut/gut_cmdln.gd -gdir=res://test -ginclude_subdirs -gexit
```

418 test cases (GUT 9.6) across: FSM, mana ramp, serialization/resume,
determinism, AI contract, all AbilityLibrary keywords, trigger queue (INLINE vs
QUEUED), multi-sided topology, command replay, and leak detection.

CI runs four gates on every push: compile check, unit tests (failing if zero
tests are collected or any test script is skipped), an ObjectDB leak gate, and
gdlint.

Dual-licensed:

- **AGPL v3.0** — free for open-source projects. Network use (server-side)
  requires releasing your product's source under the AGPL.
- **Commercial** — closed-source and server-side use without AGPL obligations.
  Contact **islasjavieralf@gmail.com**.
